recipes.title: Ropa Vieja
recipes.description: Cuba's national dish: shredded flank steak slowly braised in a tomato, pepper, and onion sofrito with wine and olives.
steps: Place flank steak in a pot with broth, half the onion, bay leaves, and a pinch of salt. Simmer 90 minutes until very tender.
Remove the steak, reserve the broth, and shred the meat with two forks along the grain.
Heat olive oil and sauté remaining onion and bell peppers until very soft, about 10 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, oregano, and tomato sauce; cook 5 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in white wine and let it reduce by half.
Add shredded beef, 250 ml reserved broth, and olives; simmer 30 minutes uncovered.
Adjust salt and serve over white rice with black beans and fried plantains.

recipes.title: Picadillo
recipes.description: Cuban ground beef hash with raisins, olives, and capers in a sweet-savory sofrito — humble, fast, and beloved.
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet and sauté onion and bell pepper until soft, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, and oregano; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add ground beef, breaking it up, and brown for 5 minutes.
Pour in white wine and let it reduce for 2 minutes.
Stir in tomato sauce, bay leaf, raisins, olives, and capers.
Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes until thickened and the flavors meld.
Discard bay leaf, adjust salt, and serve over white rice with fried sweet plantains.

recipes.title: Cubano
recipes.description: Pressed Cuban sandwich layering roast pork, ham, Swiss cheese, pickles, and mustard on lightly buttered Cuban bread.
steps: Slice the Cuban bread in half lengthwise without cutting all the way through.
Spread a generous layer of yellow mustard on the inside of both halves.
Layer roast pork on the bottom, then ham, then Swiss cheese.
Top with pickle slices and close the sandwich.
Butter the outside of the bread on both sides.
Place on a hot panini press or skillet with a heavy weight on top, pressing firmly.
Toast 3–4 minutes per side until the bread is deeply golden and the cheese is fully melted. Cut on a diagonal and serve.

recipes.title: Moros y Cristianos
recipes.description: Cuba's classic black beans and rice cooked together with sofrito until perfumed and unified — a side that anchors any meal.
steps: Drain soaked beans, place in a pot with 1.5 liters water and bay leaves. Simmer 60 minutes until tender. Reserve cooking liquid.
Heat olive oil in a heavy pot. Sauté onion and bell pepper until soft, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, and oregano; cook 1 minute.
Add rice and stir to coat with the sofrito for 1 minute.
Pour in beans with 700 ml of their cooking liquid, vinegar, and salt.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to lowest heat, cover tightly, and cook 20 minutes without lifting the lid.
Rest 10 minutes off heat, fluff with a fork, and serve.

recipes.title: Lechón Asado
recipes.description: Cuban roast pork shoulder marinated overnight in mojo of sour orange, garlic, and oregano, then slow-roasted to crispy-skinned glory.

ingredients.name: sour orange juice (or 2 parts lime + 1 part orange)

steps: Score the pork skin in a crosshatch pattern, cutting through fat but not the meat.
Whisk sour orange juice, garlic, olive oil, oregano, cumin, salt, and pepper into a mojo marinade.
Pierce pork all over with a sharp knife and rub the mojo into every cut and crevice. Marinate overnight.
Place pork in a roasting pan with onion and bay leaves. Cover with foil.
Roast at 150°C (300°F) for 4 hours, basting occasionally.
Uncover, increase heat to 220°C (425°F), and roast 30 minutes more until skin is crisp and deeply golden.
Rest 20 minutes, then shred the meat. Serve with rice, black beans, yuca con mojo, and fried plantains.

recipes.title: Pastelitos
recipes.description: Cuban guava and cream cheese puff pastries — flaky, golden, and finished with a glossy sugar glaze.
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line baking sheets with parchment.
Roll puff pastry slightly to flatten, then cut into 10 cm squares.
Place a small slab of guava paste and a 2 cm cube of cream cheese in the center of half the squares.
Top each with another square, pressing edges firmly to seal, then crimp with a fork.
Brush the tops with beaten egg.
Bake 20 minutes until puffed, golden, and crisp.
Meanwhile simmer sugar with water for 2 minutes to make a glaze. Brush over hot pastelitos and let set before serving.

recipes.title: Tostones
recipes.description: Twice-fried green plantain rounds, smashed and crisped — a quintessential Cuban side served with garlic mojo.
steps: Score plantain skin lengthwise and peel. Slice into 3 cm thick rounds.
Heat oil to 160°C (320°F) and fry plantain rounds for 3–4 minutes until pale golden but tender. Drain on paper towels.
Smash each piece flat with the bottom of a glass or tostonera to about 5 mm thickness.
Whisk garlic, olive oil, lime juice, and salt to make a quick mojo dip.
Briefly dip the smashed plantains in lightly salted water, pat dry — this helps them crisp.
Increase oil to 190°C (375°F) and fry tostones again until deeply golden and crisp, about 2 minutes.
Drain, salt immediately, and serve hot with the garlic mojo on the side.

recipes.title: Yuca con Mojo
recipes.description: Tender boiled cassava drenched in a hot garlic-citrus mojo with onions — silky, fragrant, and unmistakably Cuban.
steps: Place yuca chunks in a pot with cold salted water to cover.
Bring to a boil and simmer 25–30 minutes until very tender and the woody centers can be removed.
Drain, remove fibrous cores, and arrange yuca on a serving platter.
Heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add onion and cook 3 minutes until softened, then add garlic, oregano, and cumin; cook 30 seconds.
Carefully pour in sour orange juice — it will splatter — and simmer 1 minute. Season with salt.
Pour the hot mojo over the yuca and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Vaca Frita
recipes.description: Cuban shredded beef pan-crisped with onions, garlic, and lime until caramelized at the edges — drier and crispier than ropa vieja.
steps: Place flank steak in a pot with water to cover, half the onion, bay leaves, and a pinch of salt.
Simmer 75 minutes until very tender.
Remove steak, cool, and shred with two forks against the grain.
Toss shredded beef with garlic, lime juice, salt, and cumin; let marinate 15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat until shimmering.
Add the marinated beef in a single layer; do not stir for 3 minutes so it crisps.
Add the remaining sliced onion and toss, frying another 4–5 minutes until edges are deeply browned and onions soft. Serve with rice and beans.

recipes.title: Tamal Cubano
recipes.description: Cuban tamales of fresh corn dough mixed with seasoned pork, wrapped in corn husks and simmered until tender.
steps: Sauté pork in olive oil with onion, pepper, and garlic until pork is browned and aromatics softened.
Add tomato sauce, cumin, oregano, and salt; cook 10 minutes until thick. Cool.
Blend fresh corn kernels coarsely with a little water, then mix with cornmeal and a pinch of salt into a thick batter.
Stir the cooked pork mixture into the corn dough.
Layer two soaked corn husks slightly overlapping, spoon a generous portion of the mixture into the center.
Fold husks over to form a tight rectangle and tie firmly with twine at both ends.
Simmer tamales in salted water for 1 hour, weighed down with a plate. Drain, unwrap, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Frijoles Negros
recipes.description: Cuban black beans simmered with sofrito, bay leaves, and a splash of vinegar — silky, garlicky, and the soul of the table.
steps: Drain soaked beans and place in a large pot with 2 liters of fresh water and bay leaves.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 90 minutes until very tender.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet and sauté onion and bell pepper for 8 minutes until soft.
Add garlic, cumin, and oregano; cook 1 minute.
Stir the sofrito into the bean pot along with vinegar, sugar, and salt.
Mash some beans against the side of the pot to thicken the broth.
Simmer uncovered another 30 minutes, adjust seasoning, and serve over white rice.

recipes.title: Boliche
recipes.description: Cuban stuffed eye-of-round roast filled with chorizo and ham, slow-braised in tomato wine sauce until fork-tender.
steps: Cut a deep pocket lengthwise through the center of the roast using a sharp boning knife.
Stuff with sliced chorizo and ham, packing tightly. Tie the roast firmly with kitchen twine.
Season with cumin, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Sear on all sides in olive oil in a heavy Dutch oven until deeply browned. Remove.
Sauté onion and garlic in the same pot until soft, then add tomato sauce, wine, broth, and bay leaves.
Return the roast, cover, and braise at 160°C (320°F) for 2.5 hours, turning once, until very tender.
Rest 15 minutes, snip twine, slice across the grain, and serve with the reduced sauce, white rice, and yuca.
